preamble: The shift from 'ch' to 'x' is a process of retraction, moving the sound from the front of the mouth to the back.

examples.note: 16th–17th century retraction: Old Spanish palatal [ʃ] (itself from earlier [tʃ]) backed to velar [x] (jota). The direct [tʃ]→[x] path went through [ʃ] as an intermediate stage.
In some non-standard Mandarin varieties, palatalized affricates may correspond to velar fricatives; verification of specific Sichuanese [tɕ]→[x] is pending.

sources: Norman, J. (1988). Chinese. Cambridge University Press.
